---
title: "Overview"
---

# Overview

This documentation describes the conception and development of a data
visualisation product created for the module *Visualisation and Data Science
Storytelling* (ZHAW, FS 2025/26) by Group 11 (Lara Pestrin, Jonathan Kull,
Veprim Mehmeti).

The project, **Grocery Product Placement Analysis**, analyses the Instacart
Online Grocery Shopping Dataset (2017) to uncover actionable patterns in
grocery purchasing behaviour. Effective product placement is one of the most
impactful levers available to retailers, yet decisions are often made on
intuition rather than data. This project addresses that gap: it explores which
products are bought most frequently, which products are commonly bought together
(market basket analysis), and which products show the highest customer loyalty
as measured by reorder rate. The resulting insights are communicated through an
interactive Streamlit dashboard that supports data-driven recommendations for
product placement and category organisation.

![The visualisation product development process](pics/vizproductprocess.png){#fig-vizprocess}

The methodological procedure follows the process model shown in
@fig-vizprocess [@DoemerKempf2022]. This documentation focuses on the two required components and
comprises the following sections:

- **[Project Charta](project_charta.qmd)** — context and scope, stakeholder and
  user analysis (including personas), project objectives, success criteria,
  constraints, and the visualization concept.
- **[Data Report](data_report.qmd)** — the raw and processed datasets, the data
  acquisition and processing steps, and the exploratory data analysis that
  informs the visualisations.

Setup instructions and environment requirements for running the interactive
dashboard locally are provided in the project `README.md` at the root of the
repository.
